scenario.title: BCC vs MC/DC Showdown
scenario.narrative: We return to the Autonomous Brake trial (4 variables, 16 combinations). Previously, the budget of 5 subpoenas made BCC impossible. Now, armed with MC/DC, you can win the case.
scenario.code:
if obstacle and spd50 and dry and no_brk:
    trigger_brake()

claim: For many expressions, an MC/DC suite can often be built with N+1 tests. We have 4 conditions. Pick exactly 5 tests out of 16 to prove every variable works.
charges: Select 5 logs that form valid independence pairs for all 4 conditions.
Find the single 'True' case where everything works, and pair it with 4 'False' cases.
correct_answer_explanation: Debrief: You did it. You proved the brakes were faulty using only 5 logs instead of 16. You used R1 (T,T,T,T) as the base True case. Then you isolated each condition: Obstacle via R9, Speed via R5, Dry via R3, and Override via R2. The jury is stunned. This is why aviation relies on MC/DC!
wrong_answer_explanation: Those 5 tests don't form valid MC/DC pairs for all 4 variables. Try starting with the row where everything is True.
hints: Prosecutor's Note: Find the 'Base True' log where everything works (R1).
Then find the 4 logs that flip exactly one variable to break the brakes. Pair each of them with R1.

misconceptions.explanation_md: MC/DC provides near exhaustive confidence by ensuring no condition is masked, but scales linearly (N+1) instead of exponentially (2^N). This is the gold standard for DO-178C aviation software.
